title: Tell me What you Want, What you Really, Really Want: How to Identify the Real Business Question.
summary: Executive Summary We want our work to be impactful and used by stakeholders; we keep hearing that developing data products is the answer. Here’s a step-by-step guide on how to get there. Successful data products help our collaborators decide how to improve the company’s health. To do so, before any development starts, we first need to have a shared understanding of the context around: why something needs to happen (e.g., our objective), what specifically needs to happen (e.g., our outcome), as well as by whom and how (e.g., our strategy). This information will then help everyone get on the same page, and identify concrete steps to move the needle in one specific way to help the company grow. Many Data Requests Fall Short We’ve all been there: Let’s avoid coming up with the best answer… to the wrong question! We want to avoid these scenarios by investing time up-front in learning the context around the company’s goals and growth strategy and how to connect it to each request to develop impactful data products. This blog post outlines a framework for doing so, which will help us navigate discussions with collaborators, share expertise, and build credibility. By filling out the “discovery […] The post Tell me What you Want, What you Really, Really Want: How to Identify the Real Business Question. appeared first on Locally Optimistic .
author: Irina Kukuyeva, PhD
